title: Rewrite OnboardingResponsesList to read from shared_data and shared_documents
description: OnboardingResponsesList.tsx currently queries the old tables: onboarding_requests, onboarding_consent, documents, and joins onboarding_types. It must be rewritten to query share_requests (by requester_company_id), then for each completed request join shared_data (for field values) and shared_documents → company_documents (for file paths). The view should show a structured field table using catalog labels and document download links.
acceptanceCriteria: Component queries share_requests where requester_company_id = the current user's company id
For each completed request, field values are read from shared_data.field_data JSONB
Field values are displayed using fieldLabel(key) from catalog.ts as column headers, not raw DB keys
Documents are read by joining shared_documents → company_documents for file_path and file_name
Download button generates a Supabase Storage signed URL and triggers download
Pending and expired requests are also shown (with appropriate status badges) not just completed ones
No references to onboarding_requests, onboarding_consent, onboarding_types, or documents tables remain
tsc reports no new type errors
Playwright screenshot shows the responses table with field data

steps.description: Replace the query to use share_requests
steps.details: Replace the useQuery queryFn. First fetch: const { data: requests } = await supabase.from('share_requests').select('*').eq('requester_company_id', company.id).order('created_at', { ascending: false }). This returns all sent requests regardless of status.

steps.description: For each completed request, fetch shared_data
steps.details: Filter completed requests. For each, query: supabase.from('shared_data').select('field_data').eq('share_request_id', request.id).single(). Store field_data as Partial<Record<FieldKey, string>>. Use Promise.all for parallel fetching.

steps.description: For each completed request, fetch shared_documents with company_documents
steps.details: For each completed request, query: supabase.from('shared_documents').select('company_documents(id, document_type, file_name, file_path, file_size)').eq('share_request_id', request.id). This returns the document metadata for generating download links.

steps.description: Update the table rendering to use catalog labels
steps.details: In the 'View Details' dialog, replace the hardcoded company/contact/address sections with a dynamic field table: Object.entries(sharedData.field_data).map(([key, value]) => {fieldLabel(key as FieldKey)}{value}). Import fieldLabel from '@/lib/catalog'.

steps.description: Update document download to use Supabase Storage signed URLs
steps.details: Replace the handleDownloadDocument function. Use: const { data } = await supabase.storage.from('documents').createSignedUrl(doc.file_path, 60). Then window.open(data.signedUrl). This generates a 60-second signed URL for private storage.

steps.description: Add status badges for pending and expired requests
steps.details: Show all requests in the table, not just completed ones. Add a Status column with Badge components: pending = yellow, completed = green, expired = gray. For pending rows, disable the 'View Details' button and show 'Awaiting response' instead.

steps.description: Remove all debug logging and fix types
steps.details: Remove all console.log statements. Run npx tsc --noEmit and fix all type errors. Run npx eslint --fix.

Start dev server. Log in as admin user who has sent at least one share request. Navigate to the responses tab. Take screenshot .agent/screenshots/TASK-6-responses.png. Verify the table renders with correct field labels and no console errors.

technicalNotes: PostgREST nested select syntax for shared_documents: .select('company_documents(id, document_type, file_name, file_path, file_size)')
Supabase Storage createSignedUrl requires the full file_path stored in company_documents.file_path
The old component had a lot of N+1 queries (map + async). Use Promise.all to parallelize
shared_data.field_data is typed as Partial<Record<FieldKey, string>> in database.types.ts
